title: PID Weighted-Average Line-Tracking Controller
design_question: How to keep a differential-drive robot on a black line at 0.3 m/s?
selected_approach: Error = weighted centroid of 8 IR sensors (−35 to +35 mm). L = base + PID_out; R = base − PID_out. K_p=0.4, K_i=0, K_d=8.
rationale: High K_d damps oscillation on straights. Zero K_i prevents windup during sharp corners. K_i added only for banked surfaces.

alternatives.reason_rejected: Oscillates at high speed; K_d essential.

verification_notes: FACT: 'PID Controller — Transfer Function'—K_d reduces overshoot; consistent with derivative-heavy strategy.
